truth.papers.A19.title: Effects of annealing temperature on microstructure and mechanical properties of a low density δ-TRIP steel plate via intercritical quenching-annealing process
truth.papers.A19.paper_scope: Investigates effects of annealing temperature on microstructure and mechanical properties of Fe-8.9Mn-4.6Al-0.29C low density δ-TRIP steel processed by intercritical quenching and annealing.

prediction.papers.A19.title: Effects of annealing temperature on microstructure and mechanical properties of a low density δ-TRIP steel plate via intercritical quenching-annealing process
prediction.papers.A19.paper_scope: Investigated the effects of annealing temperature on microstructure, mechanical properties, austenite stability, and deformation behavior of Fe-9Mn-5Al-0.3C low-density steel plates processed by intercritical quenching-annealing, focusing on TRIP/TWIP effects and laminated microstructure evolution.

truth.alloys.alloy_fe8_9mn4_6al0_29c.alloys_no: Actual composition from Table 1; nominal composition Fe-9Mn-5Al-0.3C used as alias.

prediction.alloys.alloy_fe8_9mn4_6al0_29c.alloys_no: Actual composition measured for the experimental steel prepared by vacuum induction furnace. Density measured as 7.34 g/cm³ by Drainage Method using Archimedes’ Principle, which is 6.7% lower than that of conventional low-carbon low-alloyed steels.

truth.processes.proc_hr.description: Hot rolled from 80 mm to 12 mm in eight passes after reheating at 1100 °C for 2 h, initial rolling temperature ~1050 °C, finishing rolling temperature ~950 °C, then air cooled to room temperature.
truth.processes.proc_iq1050.description: Reheated to 1050 °C (ferrite+austenite dual-phase region) for 1 h, followed by water quenching.
truth.processes.proc_qa620.description: After intercritical quenching, annealed at 620 °C for 1 h, then air cooled.
truth.processes.proc_qa680.description: After intercritical quenching, annealed at 680 °C for 1 h, then air cooled.
truth.processes.proc_qa740.description: After intercritical quenching, annealed at 740 °C for 1 h, then air cooled.
truth.processes.proc_qa800.description: After intercritical quenching, annealed at 800 °C for 1 h, then air cooled.

prediction.processes.proc_hr.description: Hot-rolled state: ingot homogenized and forged, then reheated to 1100°C for 2 h, hot rolled from 80 mm to 12 mm in eight passes with initial rolling temperature ~1050°C and finishing ~950°C, followed by air cooling to room temperature.
prediction.processes.proc_iq1050.description: Intercritical quenching at 1050°C: hot-rolled plate reheated to 1050°C in the ferrite-austenite dual-phase region for 1 h, followed by water quenching.
prediction.processes.proc_qa620.description: Quenching-annealing at 620°C: water-quenched plate annealed at 620°C for 1 h, followed by air cooling.
prediction.processes.proc_qa620.processes_notes: Carbides precipitated in tempered martensite region at this annealing temperature.
prediction.processes.proc_qa680.description: Quenching-annealing at 680°C: water-quenched plate annealed at 680°C for 1 h, followed by air cooling.
prediction.processes.proc_qa740.description: Quenching-annealing at 740°C: water-quenched plate annealed at 740°C for 1 h, followed by air cooling.
prediction.processes.proc_qa740.processes_notes: This condition produced the highest product of strength and elongation and the maximum uniform elongation among the QA series.
prediction.processes.proc_qa800.description: Quenching-annealing at 800°C: water-quenched plate annealed at 800°C for 1 h, followed by air cooling.
prediction.processes.proc_qa800.processes_notes: Some austenite underwent martensitic transformation during air cooling after annealing, resulting in new martensite in the tempered martensite region.

truth.processing_steps.sample_iq1050.method: Reheated to 1050 °C for 1 h, water quenching
truth.processing_steps.sample_qa620.method: Annealed at 620 °C for 1 h, air cooled
truth.processing_steps.sample_qa680.method: Annealed at 680 °C for 1 h, air cooled
truth.processing_steps.sample_qa740.method: Annealed at 740 °C for 1 h, air cooled
truth.processing_steps.sample_qa800.method: Annealed at 800 °C for 1 h, air cooled

prediction.processing_steps.sample_hr.processing_st: Cooled to room temperature after hot rolling.
prediction.processing_steps.sample_qa620.processing: Cooled to room temperature after annealing.
prediction.processing_steps.sample_qa680.processing: Cooled to room temperature after annealing.
prediction.processing_steps.sample_qa740.processing: Cooled to room temperature after annealing.
prediction.processing_steps.sample_qa800.processing: Cooled to room temperature after annealing; some austenite transformed to martensite during this air cooling.

truth.structures.sample_hr.overall_structure: Micro-laminated microstructure consisting of δ-ferrite bands and lath martensite/α-ferrite regions, with retained austenite in martensite bands.
truth.structures.sample_iq1050.overall_structu: Micro-laminated microstructure with δ-ferrite bands thinned/segmented by prior austenite grains, martensite region enlarged, retained austenite less than HR.
truth.structures.sample_qa620.overall_structur: Micro-laminated with δ-ferrite band and tempered martensite lamellar; carbides precipitated in tempered martensite; fine reverted austenite grains at lath boundaries.
truth.structures.sample_qa680.overall_structur: Micro-laminated with δ-ferrite band and tempered martensite lamellar; fine reverted austenite grains, no carbides.
truth.structures.sample_qa740.overall_structur: Micro-laminated with δ-ferrite band and tempered martensite lamellar; highest austenite volume fraction, no carbides.
truth.structures.sample_qa800.overall_structur: Micro-laminated with δ-ferrite band and tempered martensite lamellar; slightly lower austenite fraction than QA-740 due to martensitic transformation during cooling; some fresh martensite present.

prediction.structures.sample_hr.overall_structure: Laminated microstructure consisting of δ-ferrite bands and lath martensite, with retained austenite in martensite bands
prediction.structures.sample_iq1050.overall_structu: Laminated microstructure inherited from hot-rolled state; δ-ferrite bands became thinner and discontinuous; prior austenite grain grew up
prediction.structures.sample_qa620.overall_structur: Laminated microstructure consisting of δ-ferrite bands and tempered martensite lamellar with fine reverted austenite grains and precipitated carbides
prediction.structures.sample_qa620.microstructure_l: fine grains among tempered martensitic laths
prediction.structures.sample_qa680.overall_structur: Laminated microstructure consisting of δ-ferrite bands and tempered martensite lamellar with fine reverted austenite grains
prediction.structures.sample_qa680.microstructure_l: fine grains among tempered martensitic laths
prediction.structures.sample_qa740.overall_structur: Laminated microstructure consisting of δ-ferrite bands and tempered martensite lamellar with fine reverted austenite grains
prediction.structures.sample_qa740.microstructure_l: fine grains among tempered martensitic laths
prediction.structures.sample_qa800.overall_structur: Laminated microstructure consisting of δ-ferrite bands and tempered martensite lamellar with fine reverted austenite grains and new martensite formed during air cooling
prediction.structures.sample_qa800.microstructure_l: fine grains among tempered martensitic laths
formed during air cooling after annealing

truth.interfaces.sample_hr.defect_interaction[: Interface between δ-ferrite and martensite leads to stress concentration and crack initiation; second cracks observed along interfaces.
Difference in hardness between δ-ferrite and martensite causes strain partitioning; cracks propagate into δ-ferrite band.
truth.interfaces.sample_qa620.defect_interacti: Crack initiates at δ-ferrite/tempered martensite interface, propagates into δ-ferrite band forming cleavage steps and river patterns; second cracks and laminar tears observed.
truth.interfaces.sample_qa620.interface_notes[: Hardness difference persists; strain-induced martensitic transformation causes volume expansion promoting crack closure.

prediction.interfaces.sample_qa620.interface_notes[: Stress concentration prone to occur at interface between δ-ferrite band and tempered martensite lamellar due to hardness difference; second cracks initiated at these interfaces
prediction.interfaces.sample_qa680.interface_notes[: Stress concentration prone to occur at interface between δ-ferrite band and tempered martensite lamellar due to hardness difference; second cracks initiated at these interfaces
prediction.interfaces.sample_qa740.interface_notes[: Stress concentration prone to occur at interface between δ-ferrite band and tempered martensite lamellar due to hardness difference; second cracks initiated at these interfaces
prediction.interfaces.sample_qa800.interface_notes[: Stress concentration prone to occur at interface between δ-ferrite band and tempered martensite lamellar due to hardness difference; second cracks initiated at these interfaces

truth.performance.sample_qa740.service_conditi: intended for high-strength automobile steel plates

truth.characterization_methods.sample_hr.techn: Tensile test (WDW-100E, strain rate 0.375 min⁻¹, room temperature, specimen M10×60 mm, gauge 25 mm)
truth.characterization_methods.sample_hr.key_f: HR: YS 305 MPa, UTS 1107 MPa, TE 5.6%.
truth.characterization_methods.sample_iq1050.k: IQ-1050: YS 575 MPa, UTS 929 MPa, TE 2.0%.
truth.characterization_methods.sample_qa620.ke: Mixed fracture with cleavage, quasi-cleavage, dimples; second cracks and laminar tears; river patterns and cleavage steps.
truth.characterization_methods.sample_qa680.ke: QA-680: YS 542 MPa, UTS 735 MPa, TE 45.6%.
truth.characterization_methods.sample_qa740.ke: QA-740: YS 498 MPa, UTS 725 MPa, TE 62.4%.
truth.characterization_methods.sample_qa800.ke: QA-800: YS 469 MPa, UTS 719 MPa, TE 50.4%.

prediction.characterization_methods.idx_0.technique: Field-emission scanning electron microscopy (FE-SEM) with electron backscatter diffraction (EBSD)
prediction.characterization_methods.idx_0.purpose: Microstructure observation, phase identification, austenite distribution mapping, grain boundary characterization, and GND density analysis
prediction.characterization_methods.idx_0.key_findi: Laminated microstructure of δ-ferrite bands and tempered martensite lamellar; band contrast maps; austenite volume fractions by EBSD; grain boundary distributions (AG boundaries, high-angle packet boundaries, low-angle packet boundaries, block boundaries, sub-block boundaries); austenite grain size statistics.
prediction.characterization_methods.idx_1.purpose: Measure austenite volume fraction before and after tensile tests, and estimate stacking fault energy by Rietveld refinement
prediction.characterization_methods.idx_1.key_findi: Austenite volume fractions by XRD for QA specimens; SFP and microstrain parameters for SFE calculation; XRD profiles before and after tensile deformation.
prediction.characterization_methods.idx_2.technique: Tensile testing using electronic tensile testing machine (WDW-100E)
prediction.characterization_methods.idx_2.purpose: Measure mechanical properties at room temperature
prediction.characterization_methods.idx_2.key_findi: Engineering stress-strain curves; yield strength, tensile strength, total elongation, yield-tensile ratio, and PSE obtained at strain rate of 0.375 min^-1 with rod-shaped sample size M10 × 60 mm and gauge length 25 mm, tensile axis parallel to rolling direction.
prediction.characterization_methods.idx_3.purpose: Calculate equilibrium phase fraction diagram and composition partitioning as a function of temperature
prediction.characterization_methods.idx_3.key_findi: Equilibrium phase fractions of ferrite, austenite, and cementite; A1 temperature ~617°C; elemental partitioning of C, Al, Mn between ferrite and austenite; quenching temperature 1050°C selected based on calculated results.

truth.computational_details.idx_1.analytical_m: Calculated for BCC and FCC phases; results in Fig. 8(a).
truth.computational_details.sample_qa620.analy: V_0, V_ε austenite volume fraction before/after test, ε true strain

prediction.computational_details.idx_0.validation_a: Quenching temperature 1050°C and annealing temperatures (620°C, 680°C, 740°C, 800°C) were selected based on calculated A1 temperature (~617°C) and austenite fraction evolution.
prediction.computational_details.idx_0.analytical_m: GND density from EBSD KAM maps
Burger's vector b: 2.887 Å for BCC, 2.563 Å for FCC; step size μ: 150 nm
Equations: ρGND = 2 KAMave / (μb); KAMave = exp((1/N) Σ ln KAMi)
Estimate austenite SFE from XRD Rietveld refinement parameters
Zener elastic anisotropy A, shear modulus G<111>, lattice constant a0, root mean square microstrain ε50, stacking fault probability SFP
Quantify austenite stability during tensile deformation
Equation: Vε = Vo exp(-kε), where higher k means lower austenitic stability
prediction.computational_details.idx_1.simulation_m: Python machine learning model for crystallographic boundary identification
prediction.computational_details.idx_1.validation_a: Used to identify austenitic grain boundaries, high-angle packet boundaries, low-angle packet boundaries, block boundaries, and sub-block boundaries from EBSD data, and calculate corresponding boundary densities.

truth.unmapped_findings.idx_0.value: The steel density is 7.34 g/cm³ measured by Archimedes' principle, 6.7% lower than low-carbon low-alloyed steels.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_2.value: Product of strength and elongation (PSE): HR 6.2 GPa%, IQ-1050 1.9 GPa%, QA-620 38.0 GPa%, QA-680 33.5 GPa%, QA-740 45.2 GPa%, QA-800 36.2 GPa%.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_3.value: QA-680 exhibited necking at about 25% strain, but then a second stage of uniform elongation until failure due to delayed TRIP effect.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_4.value: Work hardening rate curves show three stages: stage I sharp drop due to soft phase deformation; stage II slow decrease dominated by dislocation glide; stage III increase due to TRIP effect dominating with possible TWIP contribution.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_5.value: Grain boundary densities (length per area) from Fig. 8(b): HR - AG boundaries ~0.8, high-angle packet ~0.35, block ~0.75; IQ-1050 - AG ~0.6, block ~1.1; QA-620 - AG ~0.85, block ~0.4; QA-680 - AG ~1.25, block ~0.35; QA-740 - AG ~1.6, block ~0.25; QA-800 - AG ~1.25, block ~0.25. (units arbitrary from figure).
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_6.value: Austenite grain size statistics from EBSD: ~90% of grains <4.8 µm in all QA specimens; fine grains have higher enrichment of alloying elements and higher stability.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_7.value: The A1 temperature (cementite precipitation) is around 617 °C, which influenced the selection of annealing temperatures.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_8.value: The peritectic reaction δ-F + L → A occurs at about 1390 °C; maximum austenite fraction ~91.4% at ~970 °C in equilibrium.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_9.value: Fig. 9(a) XRD profiles before and after tensile test show peaks; the text describes intensity changes but no exact values.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_10.value: Fractures: HR and IQ-1050 were not analyzed; QA specimens all show laminar tears, second cracks, and mixed cleavage/dimple features.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_11.value: The steel is considered a typical third generation advanced high strength steel and can substitute TRIP 590T/400Y.

prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_0.value: Work hardening rate (WHR) curves of QA specimens exhibited three stages based on slope: stage I (ε < 0.01) with sharp decrease related to soft phase deformation and dislocation movement; stage II (0.01 < ε < 0.15) with slight decrease attributed to competition between dislocation gliding and hardening mechanisms including TRIP effect; stage III (ε > 0.15) with slowly increasing WHR dominated by TRIP effect and possible TWIP effect.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_1.value: Stacking fault energies (SFE) of austenite estimated by XRD Rietveld refinement: QA-620 ~36.50 mJ/m², QA-680 ~38.27 mJ/m², QA-740 ~33.29 mJ/m², QA-800 ~31.53 mJ/m², indicating TWIP effect should exist during deformation.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_2.value: Austenite mechanical stability constants (k) calculated from austenite volume fraction before and after tensile tests: QA-620 ~1.24, QA-680 ~0.56, QA-740 ~1.47, QA-800 ~1.50. Higher k value indicates lower austenitic stability.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_4.value: Products of tensile strength and total elongation (PSE): HR ~6.2 GPa%, IQ-1050 ~1.9 GPa%, QA-620 ~38.0 GPa%, QA-680 ~33.5 GPa%, QA-740 ~45.2 GPa%, QA-800 ~36.2 GPa%.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_5.value: QA-680 exhibited necking at around 25% strain due to deficient TRIP effect, but afterward underwent a second stage of uniform elongation until failure because metastable austenite produced TRIP effect after necking.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_6.value: Grain boundary densities (relative units from EBSD): for QA specimens, austenitic grain (AG) boundary density increased with annealing temperature until 800°C then decreased, while block boundary (>45°) density decreased continuously with annealing temperature.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_7.value: Fracture morphology of QA specimens: mixed fracture features with cleavage in δ-ferrite (river pattern, cleavage step), quasi-cleavage, and dimples; laminar tears observed due to laminated microstructure; second cracks initiated at interfaces between δ-ferrite and γ/α phases.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_8.value: Tensile testing conditions: strain rate of 0.375 min^-1, rod-shaped sample size M10 × 60 mm with gauge length 25 mm, tensile axis parallel to rolling direction.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_9.value: For QA-800, new martensite formed from austenite during air cooling after annealing at 800°C, as evidenced in SEM micrographs.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_10.value: The uniform elongation of all QA specimens except QA-680 was almost equal to total elongation; QA-740 had maximum uniform elongation of ~62.4%.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_11.value: Austenite grain size statistics from EBSD before tensile tests showed that around 90% of austenite grains in QA specimens were smaller than 4.8 μm.
